{"problem_standardized_math":"Consider the problem of finding the point on the parabola \\(y=\\tfrac{1}{5}(x-1)^2\\) that is closest, in the Euclidean norm, to the point \\((1,2)\\). This can be written as\n\\[\n\\min \\, f(x,y)=(x-1)^2+(y-2)^2 \\qquad \\text{subject to } (x-1)^2=5y.\n\\]\nBy directly substituting the constraint into the objective function and eliminating the variable \\(x\\), one obtains an unconstrained optimization problem. Show that the solutions of this unconstrained problem cannot be solutions of the original constrained problem."}
